test.command: cargo test -p swell-llm && cargo test -p swell-daemon
test.notes: swell-llm: 20 tests passed, swell-daemon: 62 tests passed

blockingIssues.description: init_tracer_provider() is defined but NEVER called anywhere in runtime code
blockingIssues.detail: The OTLP exporter pipeline is implemented correctly but no entry point calls init_tracer_provider(). Spans created by LLM backends go to the default no-op provider, not the configured OTLP exporter.

blockingIssues.description: OtelConfig::from_env() is defined but NEVER called anywhere
blockingIssues.detail: No configuration is loaded at runtime startup. The expectedBehavior requirement 'OtelConfig is instantiated by runtime startup or config code' is NOT met.

blockingIssues.description: LLM backends create spans but they won't be exported without init_tracer_provider()
blockingIssues.detail: anthropic.rs:207 and openai.rs:254 create spans using opentelemetry::global::tracer('swell-llm') but without first calling init_tracer_provider(), these spans go to the default no-op provider.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance that OpenTelemetry tracing requires explicit initialization via init_tracer_provider() at application startup. The infrastructure exists in swell-core/opentelemetry.rs but without explicit initialization, spans are not exported - they go to the default no-op provider.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature opentelemetry-full-runtime-export-fix implements init_tracer_provider() with OTLP gRPC exporter support and instruments LLM backends to create GenAI spans, but the critical initialization step is missing. No entry point (daemon main.rs, etc.) calls init_tracer_provider(). The Rust skill does not document this initialization requirement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that OpenTelemetry tracing in this codebase requires explicit initialization via init_tracer_provider() at application startup. Applications using swell-core opentelemetry module must call this function to export spans to configured OTLP endpoint.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: init_tracer_provider() exists but is not called anywhere except in documentation examples. Applications need to explicitly call it to export spans.
